# FilesHub

> FilesHub is a zero-cost file-storage, email, and developer-utility API. You upload a file
> over plain HTTP to `POST /api/v1/objects` with an `X-API-Key` header and get back a stable
> public URL plus a ULID. You can list, download, and delete objects, set each file's
> visibility (`public` or `private`), auto-expire files, send transactional email from
> verified domains, run background jobs, and call 40+ developer-utility endpoint groups
> (hashing, encoding, converters, QR codes, short URLs, OTP, an encryption vault, image/PDF
> processing). It is the backend used by every Aoneahsan/Zaions project. Built and maintained
> by Ahsan Mahmood.

- Product: https://fileshub.zaions.com
- Docs: https://fileshub-docs.zaions.com
- API base URL: https://fileshub.zaions.com/api/v1
- Auth: `X-API-Key: <your project key>` header on every request
- OpenAPI 3.1 spec: https://fileshub-docs.zaions.com/openapi.json
- Raw Markdown mirror (every page as .md): https://fileshub-docs.zaions.com/raw/manifest.json
- License: MIT (docs) · Author: Ahsan Mahmood (https://aoneahsan.com)

## Core concepts

- Authentication: every request carries an `X-API-Key` header. Keys are per project, with
  scopes (`read`, `write`, `email`, `email_template`, per-utility service scopes) and optional
  restrictions.
- Restrictions: a key can be marked "restricted" to only work from your own web origin,
  Android package (optionally pinned to its signing-cert SHA-256/SHA-1 via `X-Android-Cert`),
  or iOS bundle id (`X-App-Id`). This lets you ship a key in a frontend app with no proxy
  backend. Self-declared headers (Google-Maps-key trust model), not device attestation.
- Object: an uploaded file. Identified by a ULID `public_id`. Has a `mime_type`, `size_bytes`,
  a `visibility` (`public` or `private`), an optional `expires_at`, and a `url`.
- Visibility: `public` objects are downloadable by anyone at their URL; `private` objects
  require a `read` key from the same project. The integration default is `visibility: public`.

## Object API

- Upload: `POST /api/v1/objects` (write) — multipart `file` (required), optional `visibility`
  (`public`|`private`), optional `expires_in_days` or `expires_at`. Returns `201` with
  `{ public_id, project_id, visibility, mime_type, size_bytes, url, expires_at }`.
- Download / view: `GET /api/v1/objects/{public_id}` — streams the file. No key for public
  objects; a `read` key for private ones.
- List: `GET /api/v1/objects` (read) — paginated (`page`, `per_page` max 100, optional
  `visibility` filter).
- Delete: `DELETE /api/v1/objects/{public_id}` (write).

## Email API

- Send: `POST /api/v1/emails/send` (email) — raw (`subject` + `body_html`/`body_text`) or
  `template` + `variables`. One `to` per call; optional `cc`/`bcc` (<=10), `reply_to`,
  `from_name`, `attachments` (FilesHub object ULIDs, <=5), `domain` (`aoneahsan.com` default |
  `zaions.com` | `trizlink.com`), `queue` (default true => `202` + `job_id`; false => sync
  `201`), `scheduled_at`. Poll the `job_id` for delivery.
- Templates: `GET /api/v1/emails/templates` (read); `POST`/`PATCH`/`DELETE
  /api/v1/emails/templates{/slug}` (write + email_template). Slug is immutable.
- Schedules: `GET`/`POST`/`PATCH`/`DELETE /api/v1/email-schedules{/id}` + `POST
  /api/v1/email-schedules/{id}/run`. Cron or `@hourly`/`@daily`/`@weekly`/`@monthly`, IANA
  timezone, <=50 recipients per run. `PATCH {"is_active": false}` pauses.

## Jobs & status

- Jobs: `GET /api/v1/jobs` (paginated 20/max 50) and `GET /api/v1/jobs/{job_id}` (read) —
  poll a queued operation through `pending` -> `processing` -> `completed`/`failed`.
- Health: `GET /api/health` (no key) — status + database connectivity + `backend_version`.
- Version: `GET /api/version` (no key) — `{ backend_version, env }` deploy marker.
  (Both status endpoints live at `/api`, not `/api/v1`.)

## Management API (fh_pat_ access tokens)

- A server/CLI-only control plane at `https://fileshub.zaions.com/api/public/v1`, authenticated by a
  user-level access token (prefix `fh_pat_`) sent as `Authorization: Bearer fh_pat_...`. Distinct from
  per-project `fh_live_` API keys: an `fh_live_` key does NOT work here, and an `fh_pat_` token does NOT
  work on the data plane. No CORS — not callable from a browser.
- A token covers all projects (existing + future, default) or a chosen subset; out-of-scope resources
  return 404 (never 403). Errors are `{"error": {"code", "message"}}`; success is `{"data": ...}`.
- Endpoints: `GET /token` (introspect) · `GET|POST /projects`, `GET|PATCH|DELETE /projects/{project}`
  (id/public_id/slug; `?q=` search) · `GET|POST /projects/{project}/api-keys`,
  `GET|PATCH|DELETE /projects/{project}/api-keys/{id}`, `POST .../{id}/rotate`, `POST .../{id}/reveal` ·
  `GET|POST .../{id}/origins`, `PATCH|DELETE .../origins/{id}` · `POST /api-keys/lookup` (map an
  `fh_live_` key to its project + origins). Create/rotate/reveal return the plaintext key.
- Use it to have an AI coding agent map a local project's `.env` key to its FilesHub project and enforce
  origin restrictions automatically. See the Management API docs + agent workflow below.
- Supabase project vault: `GET /supabase-projects`, `GET /supabase-projects/{idOrRef}`,
  `POST /supabase-projects/{idOrRef}/reveal` — read and reveal a registered Supabase project's full
  credential set (API keys, JWT secret, Postgres connection, S3 keys) plus ready-to-paste
  React/Node/Laravel `.env` blocks. Read-only (projects are created/edited in the FilesHub admin) and
  gated by a separate, off-by-default token scope `can_manage_supabase` (a token without it gets 403,
  not 404). An unreadable secret comes back null; `has` flags report presence without decrypting.

## For AI coding agents

- Machine-readable API: https://fileshub-docs.zaions.com/openapi.json (OpenAPI 3.1; objects,
  emails, jobs, schedules, templates, version/health, AND the Management API — projects, api-keys,
  origins, lookup, supabase-projects — under the fh_pat_ BearerAuth scheme).
- Every doc page as raw Markdown: prefix any page path with `/raw/` (e.g.
  https://fileshub-docs.zaions.com/raw/api/emails-send.md). Index:
  https://fileshub-docs.zaions.com/raw/manifest.json.

## Honest limits

- FilesHub is single-region, locally-stored (not a multi-region CDN). Keep large media on a CDN
  for global edge delivery; FilesHub is built for app assets, user uploads, exports, share
  links, and transactional email.
- Default max upload size is 10 MB (configurable via `FILESHUB_MAX_UPLOAD_MB`).
- No public client SDK yet — integrate over plain HTTP. A TypeScript SDK is archived pending
  API stabilisation.
- Frontend key restrictions are header allowlisting (not cryptographic attestation): they stop
  key reuse from other apps/sites, but keep genuinely sensitive high-privilege keys on a server.
- Email sending is quota-limited per sending account; queued sends retry automatically.
